title: THE RECORDINGS — LIFELINE CUT V6

designRules.objective: Make Episode 02 feel like a live rescue operation: Hale moves while talking, misreads directions, gets tired, jokes under stress, asks if the Operator is still there, touches doors, climbs ladders, opens panels, makes mistakes, recovers, and discovers Incident Alpha through lived action instead of exposition.
designRules.choiceRule: Every visible choice group has exactly two options.
designRules.puzzlePolicy: Matching puzzle removed. Episode uses one decrypt puzzle plus operational decisions.
designRules.systemMessagePolicy: System messages are minimized and used only for terminal-critical events.
designRules.trustPolicy: Trust changes are never announced as UI text; they are felt through dialogue, hesitation, cooperation, and future flags.
designRules.v6Pass: CharacterBusy is reduced and supported by live micro-transmissions. Dialogue must emerge from physical action, fear, silence, memory, and operator pressure.

decrypt_disk_02.title: ARCHIVE DECRYPTION
decrypt_disk_02.description: Encrypted archive device detected. Hale must stay near the terminal while you recover the printed access key.
hints: THE KEY IS PRINTED AS A DIRECT HINT

events.text: Door 12 records are sealed.
I thought saying that would make me feel better.
It did not.
The relay room feels colder now.
Could be the air system. Could be me finally noticing my hands are shaking.
I am standing by the exit.
Service corridor outside. Archive Sector C should be below this deck, past the old junction.
Coffee cart used to sit there.
Bad lighting. Rivera laughing at his own jokes.
I hate that I remember the coffee cart and not why I deleted a death record.
You heard that.
Fine. Yes. I remember touching the deletion prompt.
Not clearly. More like a nightmare with the sound turned off.
Red emergency light. My hand above the confirm key.
Someone behind me.
Maybe.
No. I hate that word. Maybe keeps opening doors.
I pressed something. I do not remember why.
That is the part I am not telling well because I do not know how to say it without sounding guilty.
Moving.
The corridor outside is darker than before.
Emergency strip near the floor keeps blinking like it forgot the rest of the rhythm.
Dust on the deck.
Footprints through it.
Mine, I think.
I am choosing to believe they are mine until the station ruins that for me.
Keep the channel open. I need your eyes more than I want to admit.

e02_n02_corridor.speaker: HALE

events.text: Leaving the relay room.
Door is sticking.
Come on.
There.
I am in the service corridor. Left wall is scraped all the way down.
Something hit this passage hard enough to peel paint off metal.
Operator, you still with me?
Do not answer. Obviously you are. I just needed to ask.

e02_n02_corridor.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

e02_n03_main.speaker: HALE

events.text: Main corridor.
Fast and stupid. My favorite emergency genre.
Lights are dead overhead. Floor strips still work.
Something just rolled across the deck.
Bolt.
Probably.
I am moving before I give it a name.

e02_n03_main.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

e02_n03_ladder.speaker: HALE

events.text: Service ladder.
Miserable, but sensible.
Opening the shaft now.
It smells like hot cable insulation and old dust.
Noah hated this route.
Said it was designed by someone who had never met shoulders.
He was right.
I am halfway in and already negotiating with my ribs.
Going quiet. Both hands on the ladder.

e02_n03_ladder.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

events.text: I am at the archive door.
Paint around the lock is blistered outward.
Heat came from inside the frame.
I wiped the inspection window with my glove.
I should not have.
Rows of archive columns inside. Most dark.
One blue light blinking at the far end.
Panel is awake. Amber light. Red light.
Amber says wait.
Red says it already hates me.
I can hear the lock cycling.
If I touch this wrong, Sector C may quarantine itself.

e02_n05_scan.speaker: HALE

events.text: Scanning.
Scanner against the frame.
Metal is warm through the glove.
That is not a sentence I like.
Hold on. The scanner keeps jumping.
No, wait. I had it upside down.
Professional moment.

e02_n05_scan.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

events.text: Forcing it.
Left hand bracing the panel. Right hand on latch power.
If this arcs, I am blaming you.
Ow.
Okay.
Not dead.
Door opened, but the quarantine latch jammed halfway.
Active cache just got marked unstable.
That means some evidence may degrade while we are inside.
I am not blaming you.
I am absolutely noting the invoice.
I am inside Sector C.
Storage columns on both sides. Most are dark.
Some are blinking under frost like they are trying to breathe.
Floor crunches under my boots.
Ice crystals. Paper scraps.
A cracked ID badge with the photo burned out.
Someone taped manual labels to a few cabinets.
That is not protocol. That is what people do when they stop trusting the system.
Initials here.
E.H.
N.V.
M.C.
E.H. is me.
N.V. should be Noah Voss. Maintenance. Bad singing voice. Worse coffee opinions.
M.C... I know those initials.
I cannot find the face.
Portable storage device. Still powered.

events.title: ENCRYPTED STORAGE DEVICE
events.content:
[ENCRYPTED ARCHIVE FRAGMENT]

Archive Key Hint: ALPHA

Warning: Failed decrypt attempts may trigger quarantine response.

Status: Locked
events.caption: A portable archive device recovered inside Sector C.

events.text: It wants a key.
Label says ALPHA.
Not subtle.
Then again, neither is a locked box in a room full of dead records.
Wrong key. Archive noticed.
Do not guess. The device is not clever. It is literal, and literal machines are the worst kind of stubborn.
I am keeping the quarantine loop busy, but I can feel the terminal heating through my glove.
We get one clean retry before the system starts eating its own cache.

choices.text: ASK HALE TO READ THE DEVICE AGAIN.

events.text: Reading the device again.
Archive Key Hint: ALPHA.
Not 'Incident Alpha.' Not a sentence. Just the key word: ALPHA.
This is probably not a puzzle. It is probably a warning wearing a password.

choices.text: LET HALE STEADY THE TERMINAL FIRST.

events.text: Thank you.
My hands were starting to shake. I was pretending they were not, but the terminal has better eyesight than my pride.
I am taking ten seconds. Breathing in. Breathing out. Listening to the archive hum like it has a secret.
Ready. Try it now.

events.title: INCIDENT ALPHA PARTIAL TRANSCRIPT
events.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:15 — Unidentified movement detected.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:19 — Signal interruption.
21:20 — Casualty report created.
21:22 — Archive deletion command issued.

NOTE: Transcript incomplete. Visual, personnel, and identity data missing.
events.caption: Partial transcript recovered from the encrypted archive device.
events.message: [INCIDENT ALPHA PARTIAL TRANSCRIPT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: That opened it.
Oh.
That recording should not exist.
And yes, I know how bad that sounds.
Visual data missing. Personnel data missing. Identity data missing.
It is a skeleton with half the bones removed.
21:22. Archive deletion command.
That is the part I remember touching.
Confirm key under my finger. Red light on my hand.
I do not remember why.
Operator, I need you to understand something.
If I start protecting myself instead of the truth, push back.
Either I failed to delete it,
or someone made sure I remembered failing.
I remember sitting at a terminal. Emergency lights. Red reflection on the screen. My hand shaking before I confirmed the deletion.
There was a voice behind me. I cannot remember the words. I remember the tone.
It was calm.
That is the part that bothers me. Panic makes sense. Orders make sense. Calm means someone thought they were right.
That missing reason is starting to feel bigger than the file.
Right.
Moving.
Camera analysis terminals are on the next row.
I would like the visual trace not to be me.
I am aware hope is not a method.
Crossing the storage rows now.
Too many columns.
Too many blind spots.
I keep expecting someone to step out and ask why I am late for shift.

e02_n09_camera_access.speaker: HALE

events.text: Crossing rows.
The floor is slick here.
Careful.
I said careful to myself.
That is where we are emotionally.
Camera terminal ahead. Screen is cracked but alive.

e02_n09_camera_access.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

e02_n10_hale_inspect.speaker: HALE

events.text: I hate this plan.
Which probably means it is useful.
Expanding raw frame now.
This is not like watching footage. More like staring into broken glass and hoping the cuts line up.
My eyes are watering.
Not fear.
Probably fear.

e02_n10_hale_inspect.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

e02_n10_auto_analysis.speaker: HALE

events.text: Automated analysis.
Cold. Fast. Impersonal.
Honestly, that may be kinder.
Running it now.
Screen says under a minute.
Unless the archive decides to develop feelings.

e02_n10_auto_analysis.objective: Start a real-time interruption for ARCHIVE.

events.title: CORRIDOR CAMERA ANALYSIS
events.content:
SOURCE: Door 12 archive feed
Visual frame unrecoverable.
Automated analysis result:
- One suited figure detected.
- Suit outline compatible with ECHO engineering issue.
- Right-hand object: manual override handle.
- Timestamp damaged.
- Identity confidence: 64%
- Secondary shadow detected behind subject. Classification failed.
events.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted camera data. No image recovered.

events.text: Sixty-four percent.
Enough to make me a suspect. Not enough to make it proof.
The system is pointing at me, but the hand is shaking.
But there is something else. Secondary shadow behind the subject. Classification failed.
If that figure is me, then someone or something was behind me when the door opened.

e02_n12_override_search.speaker: HALE

events.text: There should be an override ledger beside the camera terminal.
I am checking it now. This terminal keeps dropping to read-only, like it wants to be helpful but not legally responsible.

e02_n12_override_search.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

events.text: Could the source be me?
Maybe. I hate that the honest answer is maybe.
But if it was me, why redact the source and leave a Hale-shaped outline in the camera analysis?
Either I was protecting someone, or someone was careful enough to build a trail that ends at my feet.
My current theory? Someone used my access after I lost time. I do not like that theory. I like the alternatives less.
If I am wrong, you need to catch it. I am too close to this.

e02_n13_alt_source.speaker: HALE

events.text: Searching for non-human source patterns.
I am filtering command signatures against system processes. If the source was a person, it should leave a user trail. If it was not, it leaves shape without a name.

e02_n13_alt_source.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

events.text: We have four things now.
Partial transcript.
Hale-shaped figure.
Valid authorization from nowhere.
Return signal before the channel opened.
I do not like how neatly those line up.
There is a deletion chain in the command log.
There is also an emergency note tied to the wake command.
Both are blinking at me.
I miss when lights just meant lights.
Your call.

e02_n15_deletion_chain.speaker: HALE

events.text: Tracing deletion chain.
I am following the command timestamps backward. This is not opening a file. This is pulling a thread and hoping the wall does not come with it.

e02_n15_deletion_chain.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

e02_n15_emergency_note.speaker: HALE

events.text: Opening emergency note.
I already regret saying that out loud.
The terminal is fighting me. Not locking me out. Delaying me.
That is somehow worse. A lock is honest. A delay is a decision.

e02_n15_emergency_note.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

events.title: EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE
events.content:
Emergency Backup Channel

7.14 MHz

Use only if primary communication systems fail.

Warning: Do not open if unauthorized return signal is detected.

NOTE: unauthorized return signal recorded before channel wake completion.
events.caption: Recovered emergency channel instruction connected to Incident Alpha.
events.message: [EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: No.
Nobody was supposed to find that channel like this.
Backup channels wait for someone to call them.
That is the entire point.
This one had a return signal before the wake command completed.
That is not a backup.
That is something listening behind a locked door.
Keep it closed.
Please.
I did not mean to type please.
Leave it in. It is accurate.
Good.
Thank you for not making me beg. I was ready to, which is not a sentence I enjoyed discovering about myself.
The note says not to open it if a return signal already exists.
We already have one.
And if you are reading my messages, Operator, part of this conversation is already crossing the same relay path it used.
That thought is going to keep me awake. Assuming we get to keep having awake.

choices.text: LET HALE REST FOR A MINUTE.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: THANK YOU FOR OPENING THE DOOR
No. Close it. Close it now.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Operator, listen to my voice. Not the channel. Mine.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Whatever answered is using the relay carrier as a handle. If we leave it open, it learns the shape of our signal.
events.speaker: HALE
UNKNOWN
events.text: TOO EARLY
Sealed. Partially.
And before you ask, no, 'partially sealed' is not a comforting technical phrase. It means the bad thing is delayed, not solved.
I am angry with you. That can be true while I am still trying to keep you alive.
We keep moving. Personnel records next. I need to know who else the station thinks is dead.
And I need to know whether it already wrote my name down.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: I KNOW YOUR SIGNAL NOW
What did you just do?
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No. Do not answer. I know what you did.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You gave it three more seconds. That may not sound like much until something starts measuring you back.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I do not know how much it learned. That is the problem.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Personnel records. Now. Before it learns anything else from us.
events.speaker: HALE

e02_end_personnel.speaker: HALE

events.text: Personnel records are on the next deck.
If Incident Alpha is the wound, personnel is where the station wrote down who bled.
I am not ready for that.
So obviously we are going.
Route should take seven minutes if the stairs are clear. Longer if the station wants to be dramatic.
I am going quiet. Battery conservation, and also because I need a minute before I see another list of names.

e02_end_personnel.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

e02_end_rest.speaker: HALE

events.text: A minute?
I forgot people can offer those.
Okay. I am sitting against the archive wall. Sixty seconds. No heroics. No touching cursed channels.

e02_end_rest.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

e02_end_rain_walk.speaker: HALE

events.text: Rain was the first thing I missed after month two on ECHO.
Not sunlight. Not open air. Rain. The useless sound of water hitting something that was not trying to kill you.
There was a window in the crew lounge that faced Earth. Noah used to sit there during storms we could not hear.
He said if he watched long enough, he could imagine the sound.
I told him that was sentimental nonsense.
I would like to be that stupid again.
I am moving to personnel now. Quiet for the walk. Keep the channel ready.

e02_end_rain_walk.objective: Start a real-time interruption for HALE.

events.text: At the junction.
And I was wrong.
Not completely wrong. Just the useful kind of wrong.
The old route is blocked by a collapsed ceiling panel.
There is a main corridor ahead. Faster. Exposed.
Service ladder to my right. Slower. Miserable. Probably safer.
I can hear something tapping in the main corridor.
Could be loose cable.
I am not betting my teeth on that.
Made it.
Barely.
A ceiling panel dropped behind me halfway through.
Not on me. Which is my preferred type of structural failure.
Archive access is ahead. Door frame is lit.
I am going to pretend that is good news until proven otherwise.
Down.
Mostly intact.
My shoulders hate you a little. They will file a report.
The archive door is twenty meters ahead.
There is frost on the inspection window.
Inside frost.
That is bad, right?
Rhetorical.
Scan finished.
Good call.
There was a quarantine trigger wired into the latch.
Not lethal. Just rude enough to erase whatever we came for.
I bypassed it.
Door is opening.
Slowly.
Dramatically.
Unnecessarily.
Reached the camera terminal.
The screen is flickering through old frames.
Most are static. Some are worse than static.
There is a raw frame tied to Door 12.
I can expand it myself.
Or I can let the archive run analysis and hope the machine lies less creatively than people do.
Finished.
Had to stop twice.
First because the frame kept tearing.
Second because I thought I saw my own face.
It was not clear enough.
That did not help as much as you would think.
Analysis complete. I did not look away from the screen.
I probably should have.
I pulled the override ledger.
The terminal fought me the entire way.
It kept dropping to read-only like it wanted plausible deniability.

events.title: SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG
events.content:
Override User: REDACTED
Authorization: VALID
Source: UNKNOWN
Command Chain: Door 12 / Archive Delete / Emergency Channel Wake
External Return Signal: PRESENT BEFORE WAKE COMPLETION
events.caption: Recovered security ledger for Door 12 and connected commands.
events.message: [SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Valid authorization. Unknown source.
And one more line: external return signal present before wake completion.
That means the channel was answering before it was officially awake.
That is not a normal system state. Something answered before the system says it was awake.
There. Something rode the emergency channel wake command.
It did not open Door 12. It waited until the door was open.
That is worse. That means it was not forcing entry. It was waiting for an invitation.
And we may have given it one.
Command issued after casualty report. Before emergency channel wake completed.
Whoever deleted Incident Alpha knew someone was dead, and knew the channel was waking up.
That makes it less like cleanup and more like containment.
I am back. Something changed while I was away.
I found the personnel index terminal.
It was not where I remembered it.
Someone moved it behind a maintenance partition and locked the panel from the wrong side.
[PARTIAL PERSONNEL INDEX DETECTED]
DECEASED PERSONNEL INDEX
VISIBLE ENTRIES:
GRANT — STATUS: DECEASED
RIVERA — STATUS: DECEASED
KIM — STATUS: DECEASED
ETHAN HALE — STATUS: DECEASED
Wait.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Why is my name on this list?
events.speaker: HALE

choices.text: TELL HALE TO WAIT BEFORE OPENING IT.

events.text: I can hear the station settling around me. For a second it almost sounds like rain.
Not real rain. Metal cooling. Pipes shifting. Air moving where it should not.
Still. I miss rain.
There. Minute over. Personnel records.
If you ask about rain while I walk, I might answer. Pretend that is operationally useful.

choices.text: ASK ABOUT RAIN WHILE HE MOVES.

events.text: [PARTIAL PERSONNEL INDEX DETECTED]
DECEASED PERSONNEL INDEX
VISIBLE ENTRIES:
GRANT — STATUS: DECEASED
RIVERA — STATUS: DECEASED
KIM — STATUS: DECEASED
ETHAN HALE — STATUS: DECEASED
Operator. That is my name.
Why is my name already on this list?

choices.text: TELL HALE TO WAIT BEFORE OPENING IT.

episodeObjective: Guide Hale from the relay room into Archive Sector C, recover Incident Alpha evidence, expose the Door 12 deletion chain and 7.14 breadcrumb, and end with the personnel-index cliffhanger while preserving a real-time Lifeline-style remote-operation feel.
v6Upgrades: CharacterBusy sequences are shortened and surrounded with live micro-transmissions.
Hale talks while walking, climbing, opening doors, scanning, and operating terminals.
Dialogue avoids option-explanation phrasing; choices emerge from action and pressure.
Hale makes minor mistakes, corrects himself, asks if the Operator is still present, and jokes under stress.
System messages remain minimal; emotional state is conveyed through dialogue and hesitation.
v5UpgradesPreserved: Progress bars converted into characterBusy events with hidden wait time.
Return nodes added after travel/investigation so Hale reports what happened instead of instantly continuing.
No route is described as obviously correct; choices are framed through environment and uncertainty.
All visible choice groups keep exactly two choices.
Scenario spine preserved: Door 12 records, Incident Alpha archive, camera analysis, override source, 7.14, personnel cliffhanger.
Lore is delivered through lived environment, memory, and physical actions rather than long reports.
